prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sugarplum, a CPU is just the little “thinking spot” inside the computer box—like the busy kitchen in a big restaurant where all the recipes get stirred together. I picture it as a tiny gray-haired clerk wearing spectacles, shuffling numbers and letters faster than I ever shuffled bingo cards. When you click your mouse, it’s like you’re handing that clerk a grocery list, and he dashes off to fetch, mix, and serve up whatever you asked for on the screen. It gets warm doing all that racing around, so the computer gives it its own miniature fan—rather nice, really; even clerks deserve a breeze.
